explanation_md: `d.x = 5` modifies the inherited `x` member. `show()` prints `x=5` using that value. Then `d.y` is `20`. The output is `x=5` then `20`.

prompt_md: What happens when you write `class B : A` (no access specifier) for a `class` (not `struct`)?

options.a: It defaults to `public` inheritance — same as `class B : public A`
options.b: It defaults to `private` inheritance — public members of `A` become inaccessible outside `B`
options.c: It is a compile error — the specifier is mandatory

explanation_md: For `class` (as opposed to `struct`), omitting the access specifier defaults to `private` inheritance. This makes all inherited public members inaccessible outside the derived class. Always write `public` explicitly to get the standard open inheritance.

options.a: `Employee` does not have permission to add members with the same name as the base
options.c: The `greet()` function is not inherited because it is not virtual
options.d: `std::string` default initialization is not guaranteed to work in derived classes

explanation_md: `Employee` declares its own `name` member which *hides* (not replaces) `Greeter::name`. The `greet()` function belongs to `Greeter` and uses `Greeter::name`, which was never assigned `"Alice"`. The fix is to remove line 9 and instead assign `e.name = "Alice"` in main, or set it through the inherited member.

prompt_md: Inside a member function of a derived class, can you directly access the inherited public members of the base class?

options.a: No — you must use the base class name as a qualifier: `Base::member`
options.b: Yes — inherited public members are accessible by name without any qualifier
options.c: Only if the base class member is declared `static`
options.d: Only if the derived class re-declares the member

explanation_md: Inside a derived class member function, inherited public (and protected) members are in scope and can be used by name directly, exactly as if they were declared in the derived class itself. The `Base::` qualifier is only needed to disambiguate when the member name is hidden.
